summary.name: M12 D3D12 launch and diagnostics contract
summary.goal: Make the D3D12 -> DXMT -> WineMetal -> Metal launch path explicit, testable, and diagnosable without requiring a live commercial game run.

required_env.proves: Wine loads only the M12 DXMT D3D12 frontend and WineMetal PE bridge from the staged runtime before builtin DLLs while leaving Steam overlay renderers disabled. DXGI/D3D11/D3D10/GPU vendor stubs are not forced for the winemetal-backed D3D12 path.
Game processes can find the isolated M12 Windows DLLs from the normal Steam prefix without borrowing the M11 DXMT surface.
The PE winemetal.dll bridge opens the updated PR-head WineMetal Unix sidecar by basename through Wine's unixlib route, matching the proven DXMT winemetal loading model.
DXMT component logs and focused diagnostics write to the shared Open Logs M12 pipeline folder, not shader or pipeline cache directories.
Shader outputs remain in the shader cache and are not conflated with logs.
PSO/pipeline cache outputs remain separate from the shared diagnostic log.

pipeline_stages.flow: M12 selects the DXMT D3D12 node, deploys D3D12/DXGI/Winemetal plus D3D11 fallback DLLs, and keeps M11/M10/M9 as fallbacks.
The launcher combines Steam identity, DLL overrides, Wine DLL paths, app-local sidecars, cache env, M12 log env, and per-game compatibility env.
DXMT honors DXMT_LOG_PATH so component logs and diagnostic probes write under the per-title M12 pipeline log folder.
DXGI traces swapchain/factory/adapter entrypoints through the shared diagnostic logger.
D3D12 traces device, queue, swapchain, presenter, PSO, resource, and heap events through the shared diagnostic logger.
DXIL/DXBC lowering, root signature handling, binding manifests, PSO creation, corpus replay, and Metal compilation remain separately auditable.
The DX12-only stress exe exercises app handoff, SM6-style pressure, textures, vertices, PSOs, draws, presents, and readback diagnostics without a commercial game.

dev_gates.pinpoints: M12 launcher env, shared logging, native trace routing, source comments, and required dev gates.
M12 launch env, per-title logs, app-local sidecars, runtime deployment, and compatibility knobs.
Required DLLs, Unix sidecars, WineMetal ABI, prefix placement, and manifest drift.
DXIL parser/lowering, binding, PSO, corpus replay, and shader-engine evidence surfaces.
Buildable D3D12 runtime, staged runtime files, WineMetal sidecars, m12_game.exe, and optional live cube run.
High-pressure DX12 app behavior through the same runtime and logging contract.

source_comments.must_contain: M12 should use isolated M12 DLL path

evidence.note: M12 env tests assert app-local WineMetal sidecars, DXMT overrides, and per-title shared log routing.
PR gate stages DXMT runtime files and can run the live M12 developer cube.
Shader engine source surfaces and offline gates remain separately validated.
